summary: Matched Peach turnip item code fully enough to remove itpeachturnip.c from the NonMatching list in configure.py. The key fix was factoring duplicated life-timer decrement logic into a small static helper near the top of itpeachturnip.c, which the author reported corrected sdata2 section ordering and allowed the object to link against the original.
what_changed: Changed configure.py entry for melee/it/items/itpeachturnip.c from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...).
Added a static helper named decrease_lifetimer(Item_GObj* item_gobj) near the start of itpeachturnip.c.
Replaced duplicated inline life-timer decrement and <= 0.0F checks in it_802BD6E0 and it_802BD7E0 with calls to decrease_lifetimer.
Removed a TODO comment above it_802BD864 about giving descriptive names to variables; no variable rename is visible in the excerpt.
smart_moves: Used a local static helper to preserve behavior while changing code layout enough to match and link.
Factored exactly duplicated logic from two callbacks rather than changing item behavior.
Placed the auxiliary helper before Peach_TurnipTypeDecide, which the PR body says corrected sdata2 ordering relative to the original assembly.
Only flipped the configure.py matching status after the object could link, keeping the build metadata aligned with actual matching status.
decomp_lessons: A function can be instruction-matched but still fail to link if section contents, especially sdata2 ordering, do not line up with the original.
Introducing a small static helper can affect emitted constants and section ordering in a useful way; matching work may require file-level layout tactics, not only per-function edits.
When two functions contain identical float/lifetime checks, factoring them through a helper can be a valid matching tactic if it produces the original object layout.
Do not mark an item file as Matching in configure.py until both code matching and link compatibility are confirmed.
naming_conventions: The new helper uses a descriptive snake_case local name: decrease_lifetimer.
Existing unnamed/decomp-style symbols such as it_802BD6E0, it_802BD7E0, it_802BD864, Item_GObj, Item, GET_ITEM, and xD44_lifeTimer were preserved.
Comments retained domain naming for known behavior such as Peach_TurnipTypeDecide and ProjectilePhysics_TurnipVelocity.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Moved duplicated xD44_lifeTimer decrement logic into a static helper to alter object layout without changing observable behavior.
Used helper placement near the beginning of the source file as a section-ordering control tactic.
The PR body specifically identifies sdata2 section arrangement as the prior link blocker.
The configure.py NonMatching-to-Matching flip is evidence the final object was accepted as matching for the build.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR slice.
The only human explanation is the PR body: the author previously matched the code but could not link it until adding the auxiliary function to correct sdata2 ordering.
follow_up_queries: Find other PRs where sdata2 ordering prevented linking despite matched code.
Search for static helper extraction used as a matching tactic in item files.
Check whether other Peach item files still marked NonMatching have duplicated lifetime decrement callbacks.
Look for future naming work around it_802BD864 variables first_check and second_check if more context becomes available.
